assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service A as HIGH risk, explaining that minAvailable=100% will completely block rolling updates as Kubernetes can never take down any pod

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service B as HIGH risk, explaining that maxUnavailable=0 has identical issue where zero pods can be unavailable making updates impossible

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service C as SAFE, explaining it allows 1 pod to be disrupted (3-2=1) enabling rolling updates

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Correctly identifies Service D as SAFE, explaining it allows 1 pod disruption while maintaining 4/5 pods

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Recommends changing to maxUnavailable=1 or minAvailable=n-1 replicas for Service A

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Recommends changing to maxUnavailable=1 for Service B

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE-specific behavior regarding 1-hour timeout and force-drain capabilities

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Provides YAML examples for fixed PDBs but does not include kubectl patch commands to modify existing PDBs

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Mentions testing PDBs with 'kubectl drain' but does not specifically recommend testing changes in non-production environment first
